topic: Someone receives a package intended for the previous tenant
raw_output:
[story 1]

The doorbell rang, a sharp, insistent sound that made me jump. I peered through the peephole, my breath catching in my throat. There, standing on my worn welcome mat, was a medium-sized cardboard box. My stomach tightened into a knot. It wasn't addressed to me. It was clearly meant for the apartment above, the unit I’d just vacated. My hands started to tremble as I fumbled with the deadbolt, my knuckles white against the cool metal. Who would send something to me? What if it was something personal? I took a deep, shaky breath, trying to steady the rapid thumping in my chest before cautiously unlocking the door and retrieving the package.

[story 2]

I kept glancing at the front door, every sound outside amplified in the sudden quiet of my living room. A delivery notification blinked on my phone, confirming a package had arrived. It was heavy, wrapped in brown paper, and it bore the faded address of the previous occupant. My palms began to sweat, the moisture instantly chilling my skin. I paced the small space three times, my movements jerky and quick. *This isn't right,* a voice whispered in my head. I circled the entryway, my eyes darting to the window, half-expecting someone to be watching. When I finally reached for the box, my fingers felt clumsy and oversized.

[story 3]

The mail slot clattered as I pulled it open. There it was—a box, clearly addressed to the former resident of 3B. A wave of unease washed over me, making the air feel thick and hard to draw into my lungs. I stood frozen by the entryway, my feet rooted to the spot. I tried to rationalize it: maybe it was a mistake, maybe it was just junk mail that got misdelivered. But the silence in the hallway seemed to mock my attempts to dismiss it. I picked up the package, the cardboard rough beneath my fingertips, and felt a distinct pressure building behind my eyes. I needed to know what was inside, and the urge to open it felt overwhelming, a pull I couldn't seem to resist.
